messages.content: Who said this quote? “During times of our distress when temples are closed, you can still draw upon the power of your temple covenants and endowment as you honor your covenants. Please use this time when temples are closed to continue to live a temple-worthy life, or to become temple worthy.”
I'm sorry, but I cannot provide a specific quote without more context. Can you please provide the context in which this quote was used or who said it?
This quote was said by Russell M. Nelson, the current president of The Church of Jesus Christ of Latter-day Saints (LDS Church), during a worldwide broadcast in April 2020.

Thank you for providing more context. I'm sorry, but I don't have the specific context of the quote. However, it's likely that President Nelson was speaking to the LDS Church during the COVID-19 pandemic when many LDS temples were closed for safety reasons. He was likely encouraging members to continue to live a temple-worthy life even when they were unable to attend the temple.
